channel.title: SSbusinessAndMarket
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from SSbusinessAndMarket, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: For 250 years, work defined American identity. That era is ending
item.description: AI is not only disrupting jobs but also destabilizing the work-centered identity that shaped American life, prompting a need for new concepts.

item.title: How SK Hynix quietly executed the second-largest U.S. share sale by powering the AI boom
item.description: With a $26.5 billion Nasdaq debut, the South Korean manufacturer completes a long journey from memory-chip also-ran to AI powerhouse.

item.title: A 12-person PR firm represents De Niro, Pacino, and billion-dollar clients. Its founder says staying small is the secret
item.description: The RMG founder's default answer to prospective clients is 'no,' which has helped grow revenue more than fivefold in five years without adding staff.

item.title: Fans buy small, affordable items to feel closer to celebrities like Erling Haaland and Taylor Swift
item.description: Brands profit as fans purchase inexpensive items linked to their idols, even without direct endorsements.
